source.definition: The fifth month of the Gregorian calendar, marking the spring season in the northern hemisphere.

explanation.sourceText: The English word "May" derives from the Latin month name 'Maius', which is likely named after Maia, the Roman goddess of fertility.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „May” provine din numele latin al lunii 'Maius', care probabil se referă la zeița romană Maia, zeița fertilității.

target.definition: Luna a cincea a calendarului gregorian, marcând primăvara în emisfera nordică.

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian word "mai" comes directly from the Latin month name 'Maius', which refers to the Roman goddess Maia.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul românesc „mai” provine direct din numele latin al lunii 'Maius', care face referire la zeița romană Maia.

examples.sourceText: May is often the warmest spring month.
examples.targetText: Mai este adesea cea mai caldă lună de primăvară.

examples.sourceText: The first of May is a holiday in many countries.
examples.targetText: Primul mai este o sărbătoare în multe țări.

learningTips.text: The Romanian word 'mai' sounds just like the English month 'May'—easy to remember since they refer to the same month.
Remember that 'May' comes from Maia, the Roman goddess of fertility—think of flowers blooming in May to connect the name.
In Romanian, 'mai' can also mean 'more' as an adverb, but here it means the month—context will help you tell the difference.
Imagine in May, flowers bloom 'more'—the English word 'more' sounds similar to 'mai', helping you recall the Romanian word.
